run_metadata.notes: Second data-integrity pass on 2026-03-05. Focused on wallet/transaction constraints, missing RLS analogues, and enum drift between Prisma schema and application code. Prior run (data-20260305-131933) identified migration gaps; this run audits the application-layer validation against those gaps.

agent.name: schema-auditor
agent.role: Find schema mismatches, missing RLS policies, migration gaps, data constraint violations, type drift between code and database, and validation gaps.

findings.title: Wallet balance updated without DB transaction — concurrent tips can corrupt balance
findings.description: tip.service.ts credits the recipient wallet and debits the sender wallet in two sequential Prisma calls without wrapping them in a $transaction. Under concurrent requests, one update can read a stale balance before the other commits, causing double-credits or missed debits.

proof_hooks.summary: Two separate prisma.wallet.update calls with no transaction wrapper in tip.service.ts

findings.impact: Concurrent tip submissions can result in incorrect wallet balances — funds credited twice or not debited — causing financial inconsistencies that require manual reconciliation.

suggested_fix.approach: Wrap the sender-debit and recipient-credit updates in a single prisma.$transaction([...]) call. Add optimistic concurrency with a version field or use SELECT ... FOR UPDATE via raw SQL if Prisma transactions are insufficient.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Transaction rollback must be tested to confirm it handles partial failures correctly.

history.notes: Finding created during second data-integrity audit pass on 2026-03-05.

findings.title: Payout amount validation allows zero-value and negative payouts
findings.description: CreatePayoutDto uses @IsNumber() and @Min(0) but not @Min(1). A payout request with amount: 0 passes DTO validation and reaches the database. The Prisma schema has no check constraint on payout amounts. Negative amounts are blocked by @IsPositive() but zero is not.

proof_hooks.summary: Prisma Payout model has no check constraint for amount > 0

findings.impact: Zero-value payout requests create database records and may trigger downstream Stripe API calls with a $0 amount, which Stripe rejects with an error, causing unhandled exceptions in the payout service.

suggested_fix.approach: Change @Min(0) to @Min(1) in CreatePayoutDto.amount. Add a Prisma migration to add a check constraint: ALTER TABLE Payout ADD CONSTRAINT payout_amount_positive CHECK (amount > 0).
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Migration may fail if any existing rows have amount = 0 — run a cleanup query first.

history.notes: Finding created during second data-integrity audit pass on 2026-03-05.

findings.title: GigApplicationStatus enum in Prisma not exhaustively handled in applications.service.ts
findings.description: The Prisma schema defines GigApplicationStatus with values PENDING, ACCEPTED, REJECTED, WITHDRAWN. The applications.service.ts switch statement handling status transitions is missing the WITHDRAWN branch — falling through to a default that throws. A user withdrawing an application after a service restart would receive an internal server error.

proof_hooks.summary: Switch on GigApplicationStatus missing WITHDRAWN case in applications.service.ts

findings.impact: Users attempting to withdraw a gig application may receive a 500 error. The record is not corrupted but the operation fails silently from the user's perspective.

suggested_fix.approach: Add a WITHDRAWN case to the status transition switch in updateStatus(). Consider using TypeScript exhaustive checks (assertNever pattern) to catch missing enum cases at compile time.

history.notes: Finding created during second data-integrity audit pass on 2026-03-05.

next_actions.action: Wrap tip wallet updates in prisma.$transaction to prevent balance corruption under concurrency
next_actions.rationale: P1/major bug with evidence. Financial data integrity risk. Fix is small effort with low regression risk.

next_actions.action: Change CreatePayoutDto.amount @Min(0) to @Min(1) and add DB check constraint
next_actions.rationale: P1/major bug. Trivial fix. Zero-value payouts can cause unhandled Stripe API errors.

next_actions.action: Add WITHDRAWN case to GigApplicationStatus switch and add exhaustive type checks
next_actions.rationale: P2/minor debt. Trivial fix. Prevents 500 errors for users withdrawing applications.
